vulnerability.reference: Watermark design disclosure allows adversaries to craft targeted perturbations, removing the detectable signature.
vulnerability.generated: Verification services depend exclusively on watermark integrity, leaving no fallback to detect forgery when watermarks are stripped.

impact.reference: Verification services become unreliable, triggering a chain reaction of eroded trust in digital content attribution.
impact.generated: Undetected machine-generated text spreads, breaking provenance verification and enabling large-scale automated disinformation.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial perturbations exploiting known watermark designs strip signatures, exposing verification services to unreliable detection, eroding trust in digital content attribution.
dose_response.generated: Watermark removal and evasion attacks strip LLM text watermarks, through verification services that rely solely on watermark integrity, triggering their inability to detect forgery, leading to undetected machine-generated text spreading and enabling large-scale disinformation.
